tldr: Headlines split between two parallel tracks: the criminal sanction (ankle tag, guilty verdict, prison sentence, court-ordered monitor) and the defiant candidacy launch (run for president, cleared, path to power). Germany’s outlets alone pivot to the bilateral foreign-policy angle; Straits Times uniquely leads with polling rather than verdict or campaign.

frames.sub_frame: judiciary cedes final verdict to voters

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline in the corpus to label Le Pen a ‘conservative presidential candidate’ rather than ‘far-right’ — every other outlet uses ‘far-right’ or a neutral noun. Also frames the ruling as the court ‘clearing a path’ rather than ‘upholding a conviction,’ reversing the legal emphasis visible in all other headlines.

Headline (‘Brussels gives cows a promotion’) is entirely off-topic — it covers the EU’s livestock strategy reclassification. The Le Pen story appears only in the podcast body. Politico’s headline contributes zero Le Pen framing to the headline corpus; its body-only signal is a structural headline–body divergence case.

Sole headline leading with electoral polling (‘Opinion polls see France’s Le Pen winning 2027 election despite guilty verdict’) rather than verdict or campaign launch — uniquely forward-looking among all corpus titles.

bottom_line: The headline layer delivers a near-uniform crime-plus-defiance double bill — conviction and ankle tag on one track, presidential candidacy launch on the other — with Fox News the sole outlier relabeling Le Pen ‘conservative’ and Politico Europe the only outlet whose headline is entirely disconnected from the story.
